channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Flood gates are dropped from a plan to protect the Jersey Shore’s back bays from catastrophic storms
item.description: Huge gates at the mouths of three inlets and barriers across bays are no longer part of a plan to protect New Jersey’s back bays from the type of catastrophic flooding they endured during Superstorm Sandy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Joe Manchin calls Democratic Party ‘toxic,’ blames progressives
item.description: Sen. Joe Manchin, I-W.V., blamed progressives for turning the Democratic Party "toxic" as he prepares to retire from the Senate at the end of this term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US agriculture primed to be next frontier in cybersecurity in new year, experts, lawmakers say
item.description: Agriculture is a major economic driver in the U.S., but it is just as prone to foreign cyber threats as more widely reported areas of the U.S. economy and security state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Isolated Chicago communities secure money for a coveted transit project before Trump takes office
item.description: Decades after a former Chicago mayor pledged to extend transit to the city’s far South Side, the federal government is committing to the massive project just before President Joe Biden leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Mcmurray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NJ rep. says it's alarming Feds can't bring down one drone
item.description: Chris Smith is disappointed by what he sees as a lack of transparency by the Biden administration over the New Jersey drone flap.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Government handling of drones a 'catastrophe': Elizondo
item.description: The FAA temporarily banned drone flights in areas of New Jersey and New York, where critical infrastructure is located.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump says she's removing herself from consideration to be Florida senator
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lara Trump, the daughter-in-law of President-elect Donald Trump, said Saturday that she's removing herself from consideration to be a Florida senator — ending speculation that she could replace Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump backs MAGA loyalist Joe Gruters for RNC treasurer
item.description: After the 2021 Capitol siege, Gruters, a former chair for the state GOP, was one of 17 state lawmakers who voted to contest President-elect Joe Biden’s Electoral College win.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump selects ex-DHS official for key DOJ post
item.description: He was previously an acting general counsel and chief of staff at DHS. Before that role, Mizelle was the counsel to the deputy attorney general at the DOJ.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Rockin’ around the congressional Christmas tree
item.description: Although the House of Representatives rejected the 1,547-page interim spending bill, lawmakers reached an agreement Friday to avoid a government shutdown before the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrats condemn ethical lapses by Thomas, Supreme Court justices
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee condemned the Supreme Court for an “ethical crisis of its own making” in a new report released Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps 'Apprentice' producer, Mark Burnett, as special envoy to the United Kingdom
item.description: Mark Burnett, the power producer who helped reintroduce Donald Trump to a national television audience with “The Apprentice,” is being tapped by the president-elect as special envoy to the United Kingdom in his upcoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What is next for Florida's insurance market as CFO Patronis steps down
item.description: He is the state's top insurance official, but Florida's Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis is stepping down in a few months to run for Congress. Governor Ron DeSantis will appoint a temporary replacement to the CFO position, as Florida residents continue to face a fragile insurance market.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) marked the achievement after the Senate confirmed Serena Raquel Murillo to serve as district judge for the Central District of California.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: White House pressed on Biden's absence during funding talks
item.description: White House Press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre was pressed Friday about why President Biden has stayed out of the public eye when it comes to government funding talks that are gripping Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House tie in peril after judge rules DFL lawmaker can’t be sworn in
item.description: A district court judge on Friday granted a challenge to Democratic Rep.-elect Curtis Johnson’s residency, blocking him from taking office.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost about 2.1 million troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday's deadline to prevent a government shutdown, the Pentagon warned.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP eyes plan C that cuts Trump debt ceiling demand
item.description: House Republicans are eyeing a new spending proposal that would tee up votes on three separate measures: to fund the government, appropriate disaster relief and allocate farm assistance, three sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown. It now goes to President Joe Biden, who is expected to quickly sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP eyes plan B stopgap with separate 2-part debt limit commitment
item.description: House Republicans are eyeing an alternative government funding stopgap that excludes a debt ceiling increase, and are instead working on a commitment to raise the borrowing limit twice next year in a bid to appease President-elect Trump, two sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson spending deal throws Speakership into question as floor vote approaches
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson’s handling of an end-of-year spending deal is throwing his grasp on the gavel into uncertain territory ahead of a critical Jan. 3 Speaker vote, as some GOP lawmakers question their support for the Louisiana Republican. Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Vance meets with Johnson after Trump torpedoes spending bill
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance met with Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday night, hours after President-elect Trump torpedoed the GOP leader’s spending deal.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump said he wants to ban trans athletes from competing. The reality is more nuanced
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s campaign promise to ‘ban’ transgender athletes struck a chord with voters. It’s uncertain, however, what exactly Trump will do once his second term begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Global warming can’t be ignored, Montana’s top court says, upholding landmark climate case
item.description: Montana’s Supreme Court has upheld a landmark climate ruling that said the state was violating residents’ constitutional right to a clean environment by permitting oil, gas and coal projects without regard for global warming.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: In last-ditch effort, Democrats push to get Julie Su confirmed as labor secretary
item.description: Acting Secretary Julie Su has led the Labor Department for nearly two years, despite never getting a Senate confirmation vote. With time running out, her staunchest supporters haven't given up.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: The president of the NCAA calls for a ban on 'prop bets' in college sports
item.description: As sports betting has spread across the U.S., college athletes have increasingly faced harassment from bettors. A federal ban on wagers that concern individual performances could help, the NCAA says.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York judge says Trump is not immune from hush money conviction
item.description: A spokesperson for President-elect Trump said the case should never have been brought, called for its immediate dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump weighs in on NYC mayor, vaccines and drones in freewheeling press conference at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump told reporters that he would consider pardoning embattled New York Mayor Eric Adams, said the country was “not going to lose” the polio vaccine and weighed in on the flurry of drone sightings over New Jersey during a freewheeling press conference Monday at his Mar-a-Lago
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump says he would consider pardoning NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- President-elect Donald Trump on Monday said he would consider pardoning Mayor Eric Adams after his historic indictment on federal charges. "I think that he was treated pretty unfairly," Trump said at a press conference in Florida. "I would certainly look at it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump girds for battle with Democrats, Supreme Court over birthright citizenship
item.description: President-elect Trump, Democratic lawmakers and the Supreme Court are on a collision course over restricting birthright citizenship, which Trump has identified as a top priority for his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
